pr.title: fix(infra): add quality-gates command to pre-push hook (infra-002)
pr.author: vertz-tech-lead[bot]

testBeforeImpl.evidence: Single commit fc14d12 contains both test file creation (68 lines) and implementation changes (7 lines) with no prior commits showing test-first approach

redGreenCycle.evidence: No separate commits for red (failing test) and green (passing implementation). Cannot verify tests initially failed.

oneTestAtATime.evidence: All 3 tests (68 lines) written in single commit, likely batch-written

qualityGatesBeforeCommit.evidence: No transcript available. PR body doesn't mention running quality gates. Reviewer mentioned 'all pass' but after-the-fact.

noTypeEscapes.evidence: No @ts-ignore, as any, .skip, or --no-verify found in changes

branchCreated.evidence: Branch fix/infra-002-prepush-hook exists, proper naming convention

atomicCommits.evidence: Single commit contains test + implementation + dependency change. Should be 3 separate commits.

changesetAdded.evidence: Added yaml dependency to package.json correctly

botScriptsUsed.evidence: Commit author vertz-tech-lead[bot] with correct email format

noPushToMain.evidence: PR workflow used, merged via pull request #257, not direct push

ticketExists.evidence: References infra-002 but /workspace/vertz/tickets/infra-002.md does not exist

readTicketFirst.evidence: Ticket file infra-002.md not found in repo, cannot verify it was read

readRules.evidence: No transcript available to verify RULES.md was read

scopeCompliance.evidence: Changes match PR body acceptance criteria. 3 files changed, all related to lefthook configuration.

deviationsEscalated.evidence: No deviations detected from stated scope

developerWalkthrough.evidence: N/A - infrastructure change, not end-user feature

userOutcomeTests.evidence: Tests verify actual YAML structure and behavior, not mocks

verticalSlice.evidence: Complete fix: quality-gates added, dagger made optional, tests verify configuration

dxImprovement.evidence: Removes LEFTHOOK=0 workaround requirement, works without dagger, improves contributor experience

noEval.evidence: No eval() or new Function() in changes

noHardcodedSecrets.evidence: No secrets in diff or configuration

violations.description: Tests and implementation committed together without demonstrating red-green cycle
violations.evidence: Commit fc14d12 contains both test file (68 lines) and implementation (7 lines) with no prior test-only commit
violations.recommendation: Future work: (1) Commit test file with failing tests first, (2) Run tests to verify failure, (3) Implement fix, (4) Run tests to verify pass, (5) Commit implementation separately

violations.evidence: Single commit with all changes, cannot verify tests initially failed
violations.recommendation: Use separate commits for RED (test + failure verification) and GREEN (implementation + pass verification)

violations.description: Non-atomic commit mixing test, implementation, and dependency changes
violations.evidence: Single commit modifies lefthook.yml, adds test file, and adds yaml dependency to package.json
violations.recommendation: Break into logical commits: (1) test(infra): add lefthook config tests, (2) fix(infra): add quality-gates command, (3) chore(deps): add yaml to integration-tests

violations.evidence: Commit references infra-002 but /workspace/vertz/tickets/infra-002.md does not exist
violations.recommendation: Create ticket file in /workspace/vertz/tickets/ before starting work, commit it before creating PR

violations.description: No evidence quality gates were run before commit
violations.evidence: No transcript available, PR body doesn't mention running tests/typecheck/lint
violations.recommendation: Include quality gate output in PR body or commit message. Consider adding pre-commit hook.

summary: Infrastructure fix successfully improves DX by removing LEFTHOOK=0 workaround and making dagger optional. Changes are focused and tests are comprehensive. However, TDD process was not followed: tests and implementation were committed together without demonstrating a red-green cycle. Grade: C (functional and beneficial, but process violations). Flagged for CTO review due to TDD enforcement concerns.

positives: Tests check actual YAML structure, not mocks
concerns: TDD cycle completely skipped - major process violation
Pattern may indicate need for TDD training/enforcement
No session transcript available for audit
recommendations: Add pre-commit hooks enforcing test-first workflow
Update agent prompts to require separate commits
Add automated PR checks flagging test+impl in single commit
Preserve session transcripts for audit trail
Commit ticket files before creating PRs
